hypothesis: The full TinyStories output head should keep exact top-k as a streaming row scan, but rowwise int8 sidecars must be part of the top-k compare contract because raw accumulators are only comparable across rows when the weight scale is shared. A compact sidecar stream adds about 6.25 percent bandwidth and is still cheaper than materializing logits or keeping the full tied table on chip.

commands.cmd: sed reads of AUTONIGHT_STATUS.md and required Task 6 evidence artifacts

commands.failure: bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted

commands.cmd: sed substitution write of compact JSON artifact

profile_a_payload_only_per_tensor.description: Current v4k top1 proof style: one shared weight scale, 64 int8 payload bytes per row, and raw int32 accumulators are comparable for argmax because all rows share the same positive scale.
profile_a_payload_only_per_tensor.compare_rule: compare raw int32 accumulators, tie-break on lower token id
profile_a_payload_only_per_tensor.promotion_state: validated only for the existing single-sample v4k per-tensor proof, not for full-vocab multi-sample replay

profile_b_rowwise_sidecar_separate.description: Full-output-head planning profile: keep 64-byte int8 payload rows, pack sixteen 4-byte sidecars per 64-byte sidecar burst, and compare scaled scores.
profile_b_rowwise_sidecar_separate.compare_rule: score_q equals int32_accumulator times positive_row_scale_q, with a common activation scale omitted from argmax; tie-break on lower token id
profile_b_rowwise_sidecar_separate.promotion_state: recommended first replay profile for full-vocab rowwise int8

profile_c_materialized_logits.description: Rejected first implementation profile because exact top-k can update a small register set during the scan.

arithmetic_contract.int8_accumulator_bound_note: With 64 products of signed int8 activation and symmetric int8 weight, worst-case magnitude is about 1040384, so int32 accumulators have large headroom.
arithmetic_contract.rowwise_argmax_requirement: Per-row scales make raw accumulator order insufficient. The replay and RTL contract must compare scaled scores or use an exactly equivalent monotonic fixed-point representation.
fixed_point_replay_requirements: record sidecar decode format before RTL
record score_q fractional bits and saturation policy
prove top1 or top5 equivalence against f32 reference for deterministic samples
emit worst changed-rank rows when rowwise and per-tensor profiles disagree

validation.method: static arithmetic and contract derivation from checked-in artifacts; sed readback only
validation.hardware_block_reason: no board access in this run

decision.rationale: The sidecar stream is a small bandwidth increment, but it changes the correctness contract for top-k comparison. DDR3 integration should wait until executable replay fixes sidecar decode, score scaling, and tie-break semantics.
decision.next_gate: Recover Python, implement the recommended replay script for 8 deterministic samples, and only then decide whether full TinyStories should use per-tensor payload-only or rowwise sidecar output-head rows.
